sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old female, presents with a several-year history of acid reflux symptoms, which have worsened over the past few years.
She reports feeling burning in her chest and throat, particularly after eating certain trigger foods such as spicy, citrus, and tomato-based products.
She has been taking omeprazole 40 milligrams twice daily for approximately one year, but her symptoms are poorly controlled.
She also reports difficulty managing stress, which exacerbates her symptoms.
She has a history of anxiety and seasonal allergies.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of anxiety and seasonal allergies.
Her grandmother had esophageal cancer, and her mother had acid reflux.
She had a surgical scar on her abdomen from a twisted intestine repair at one month old.
Review of systems: The patient denies any difficulty swallowing, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, constipation, diarrhea, or rectal bleeding.
Current medications: She is currently taking omeprazole 40 milligrams twice daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: A normal physical exam was noted, except for well-healed abdominal surgical scars.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) with underlying functional etiology.
Problem list: GERD; suspected; confirmed; past; unknown
Plan:
I prescribed Nexium 40 milligrams twice daily for her GERD.
I recommended that she make dietary changes to avoid trigger foods such as spicy, citrus, and tomato-based products.
I also advised her to work on managing stress to alleviate her symptoms.
I ordered an upper endoscopy (EGD) to evaluate for potential causes of her GERD.
I will send a prescription for Nexium to her pharmacy on file.
I will have her schedule the EGD at the checkout desk and follow up with me in the office to discuss the results and review her progress.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in the office for follow-up and to discuss the results of the EGD, as well as her response to the dietary and stress management changes and Nexium therapy.
